summary.name: CaiLama
summary.type: chess analysis, training, automation and retrieval ecosystem
summary.purpose: PGN import, Stockfish analysis, LLM-assisted commentary, DGT-aware training, knowledge search, player development and reproducible artifacts
summary.master_rule: CaiLama-Master is documentation, coordination and website only; it is not a monorepo and contains no runtime implementation from subprojects.

repositories.name: CaiLama-Master
repositories.role: ecosystem documentation, website, roadmap, orchestration and local checks
rules: Do not store secrets, tokens, .env files or production credentials.
Keep runtime implementation out of the master repository.

repositories.name: CaiLama
repositories.role: main chess system for analysis, training, profiles, agent CLI, PGN, Stockfish and DGT-aware workflows
current_focus: DB hybrid access is configurable through database.access_mode values native, api and hybrid
bounded Webspace DB API status client uses protected POST /api/v1/status without SQL-over-HTTP or secret output
provider-side dump import is available through no-query/no-body append/reset endpoints that process a configured server-side .sql or .sql.gz file
provider database schemas are applied through protected PHP admin endpoints on the webspace, not through local provider DB access
PTG queue enqueue is wired; optional --run-llm-stages connects classify/analyze before personalized card generation
internal SearchAdapter is standard path for web_search and search_dwz
search_rag is suggested for research/source questions
DWZ identity linking store and CLI integration
RAG provenance in researcher and analyst flows
constraints: No live external, engine, router, database or hardware calls unless explicitly requested.
Do not expose local configuration secrets.
New domain logic should be implemented as reusable modules first.

repositories.name: CaiLama-LLM-Router
repositories.role: OpenAI-compatible LLM router with aliases, backend routing, fallback policies and operational checks
current_focus: streaming backend errors are returned as final SSE chunks
optional config hot reload through runtime.reload_config_on_request
validated backend-specific alias model mapping
JSON and Prometheus-compatible metrics output
cooldown metrics count only actual backend cooldown transitions
mypy src is clean
no active follow-up work should be started without a new user request
constraints: No chess product logic in the router.
No provider secrets in repository files.
Retrieval context belongs to CaiLama and CaiLama-Search.

repositories.name: CaiLama-Search
repositories.role: search, DWZ and RAG service with FastAPI, Meilisearch, crawler and import paths
current_focus: CaiLama-compatible API contract with normalized items, results, context and sources
explicit Meilisearch master key and dedicated API key in Docker defaults
admin service token via environment or localhost fallback
crawler source policy, robots groups, source validation and reindex tracking are tested
privacy-safe search observability endpoint is available
synthetic goldsets for search contract, DWZ search and RAG context are versioned and CLI-validated
localhost-protected goldset seeding for isolated test Meilisearch instances is available
goldset end-to-end smoke is automated through a local Docker Meilisearch instance, synthetic seeding and a scheduler-disabled API subprocess
current expansion focus is Search quality: API quality, job orchestration and optional semantic retrieval
job orchestration with CaiLama queue and training
optional semantic retrieval with evaluation data
constraints: No real Meilisearch keys, master keys, admin keys or .env files in repository files.
Live imports, crawls and external network calls require explicit instruction.
Do not rely on disabled tokenized DWZ interfaces.

interfaces.purpose: LLM access through OpenAI-compatible endpoints and role aliases
controlled search, RAG context and DWZ data
contract_notes: POST /v1/search is canonical; GET /v1/search?q=... remains compatible.
POST /v1/context accepts query parameters or JSON with query and limit.
Search responses expose native hits and normalized items/results.
Context responses expose context_blocks plus compatible context/sources.

interfaces.purpose: provider database access through a narrow PHP API, server-side CaiLama dump imports and website login through a separate auth database
contract_notes: Real credentials belong only in private webspace config outside /public.
databases.auth and databases.cailama are separate PDO configurations.
Status requires POST /api/v1/status with a Bearer token scope; there is no public GET status with API or database details.
The import endpoints accept no query parameters and no request body; authentication is through a Bearer token scope.
Provider schema endpoints accept no query parameters and no request body; authentication requires the admin scope.
Status, append import, reset import and admin use separate keys/scopes.
Reset import requires db_import:reset or admin.
Real webspace config is deployed outside the public webroot under cailama-private/api/config.local.php.
Large dump data is uploaded out-of-band to a non-public webspace folder and processed from the configured server-side file.
Missing import files are rejected and successfully imported files are deleted.
Reset imports require an explicit local allow_reset setting.
web/api_app/config.local.sample.php is a placeholder template only.

interfaces.purpose: documentation, coordination and local status checks only

now: CaiLama DB hybrid hardening after configurable native/api/hybrid access, bounded API status client and server-side provider dump import endpoints
DWZ identity linking and RAG provenance
Search expansion for recall, quality and CaiLama integration after versioned synthetic goldsets, test-index seeding and automated E2E smoke
Router is paused unless a new user request targets it
next: PTG live verification and follow-up hardening for personalized cards
Unified job orchestration for CaiLama and Search
later: unified job orchestration for import, crawl, game analysis, PTG and reindex
expansion: optional semantic retrieval layer in CaiLama-Search
